items.title: Zašto žene teže podnose vrućinu od muškaraca? Znanost ima objašnjenje
items.summary: BRITANSKI liječnik objašnjava biološke razloge zašto žene teže podnose vrućinu.

items.title: The tea in your kombucha changes more than just the taste
items.summary: Scientists discovered that kombucha’s flavor, chemistry, and antioxidant activity vary dramatically depending on the tea used to make it. Green and oolong tea kombuchas emerged as the most biologically active, while fermentation transformed each tea into a distinctly different beverage.

items.title: Scientists finally solved how H5N1 bird flu hid in dairy cows
items.summary: Researchers uncovered why H5N1 bird flu attacks cows’ udders instead of their lungs: the virus’s preferred receptors are concentrated in mammary tissue. The breakthrough could help scientists predict future bird flu jumps and spot unusual infections before they spread widely.

items.title: Hlade li nas vrući napitci tijekom vrućina? Znanost ima odgovor
items.summary: SUPROTNO uvriježenom mišljenju, vrući napitci mogu rashladiti tijelo - ali ne u svim uvjetima.

items.title: Liječnik upozorava: U ovo doba dana nemojte vježbati tijekom toplinskog vala
items.summary: VRUĆINE koje stižu donose i veći rizik od toplinske iscrpljenosti.

items.title: New brain study reveals speech learning works differently than we thought
items.summary: A new study suggests that learning and remembering speech relies more on how the brain processes sounds and sensations than on the areas that control mouth and face movements. The discovery could reshape speech therapy and help improve future brain-based communication technologies.

items.title: One of the world’s most popular weedkillers may be fueling deadly superbugs
items.summary: Researchers found that highly drug-resistant bacteria from hospitals are also resistant to glyphosate, a commonly used weedkiller. The discovery suggests that agricultural herbicides may be helping antibiotic-resistant microbes survive and spread far beyond healthcare settings.

items.title: This emerging treatment is helping people avoid knee replacement surgery
items.summary: A minimally invasive treatment called GAE is helping people with chronic knee pain get back to gardening, cycling, and other activities without undergoing knee replacement surgery. Early studies suggest the procedure can provide years of relief by reducing inflammation inside the joint.

items.title: Scientists say most people need more protein than current guidelines suggest
items.summary: A new review suggests that doing more exercise and eating more protein than current minimum recommendations may help people stay stronger, sharper, and more independent as they age. The goal isn't building a beach body—it's extending healthspan and maintaining the ability to fully enjoy li

items.title: First drug to delay onset of type 1 diabetes made available on NHS
items.summary: The immunotherpay can give children and adults three extra years before they need to use insulin.

items.title: Puberty blocker trial will help reduce harm, says Cass report author
items.summary: Dr Hilary Cass says she is "absolutely convinced that more children will be harmed if we don't do the trial than if we do."

items.title: Zašto hormoni mogu biti uzrok debljanja i što možete učiniti
items.summary: HORMONSKA neravnoteža može uzrokovati debljanje, osobito u području trbuha. Stanja poput menopauze, PCOS-a i hipotireoze utječu na hormone. Važno je potražiti liječničku pomoć za dijagnozu i liječenje.

items.title: Vježbe u bazenu za trening cijelog tijela
items.summary: VJEŽBANJE u vodi jača mišiće i poboljšava kondiciju bez opterećenja na zglobove. Idealno je za osobe s ograničenom pokretljivošću, ali i za sve koji žele poboljšati snagu, ravnotežu i zdravlje.
